steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence question. The sentence to complete asks for “one example of a neighboring species with a negative effect value.” The table lists three neighboring species alongside their effect values. The task is to read the table and select the neighboring species whose effect value is negative (below zero).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — *Elands sourfig*.: The table shows Elands sourfig has an effect value of 0.3580 — positive, not negative. ✗
  • Choice B — *creeping thistle*.: The table shows creeping thistle has an effect value of 0.2523 — positive, not negative. ✗
  • Choice C — *wild radish*.: Wild radish appears in the table as the target species paired with creeping thistle, not as a neighboring species. It has no effect value listed in the “neighboring species” role. ✗
  • Choice D — *leafy spurge*.: The table shows leafy spurge has an effect value of −0.3238 — the only negative value in the table, and it is listed as a neighboring species. ✓
D is correct.
